title: Uniformly convergent computational method for singularly perturbed unsteady burger-huxley equation
author: Daba, Duressa

steps_raw.title: Convergence analysis
steps_raw.instruction:
Lemma 4.1Discrete Maximum Principle
Suppose that the discrete function
Ψij+1
satisfies
Ψij+1≥0
, on
∂Υ
. Then
£εN,MΨij+1≥0
on
ΥN,M
implies that
Ψij+1≥0
at each points of
Υ¯N,M.

steps_raw.title: Convergence analysis
steps_raw.instruction: This lemma gives guarantee for the existence of unique discrete solution.Lemma 4.2Discrete Uniform StabilityThe solutionℑ^ij+1of the discrete problem(3.27)at(j+1)thtime level andη=min0≤i≤N{rij+1}, whereηis some positive constant satisfies∥ℑ^ij+1∥≤∥£εN,Mℑ^ij+1∥η+max{|ℑ^0j+1|,|ℑ^Nj+1|}.ProofLet define barrier functions (Πij+1)± as(Πij+1)±=Z±ℑ^ij+1,where Z=∥£εN,Mℑ^ij+1∥η+max{|ℑ^0j+1|,|ℑ^Nj+1|}.On the boundary points:(Π0j+1)±=Z±ℑ^0j+1=∥£εN,Mℑ^ij+1∥η+max{|ℑ^0j+1|,|ℑ^Nj+1|}±Θj+1(0)≥0,(ΠNj+1)±=Z±ℑ^Nj+1=∥£εN,Mℑ^ij+1∥η+max{|ℑ^0j+1|,|ℑ^Nj+1|}±Θj+1(N)≥0.Now, on the discretized spatial domain ΥℓN:£εN,M(Πij+1)±=£εN,M(Z±ℑ^ij+1)=(−σ(ρ)ρ−3ω1ϖi−1j+12−ω2ϖij+1+ω1ϖi+1j+12+ℓω1ri−1j+1)(Z±ℑ^i−1j+1)+(2σ(ρ)ρ+2ω1ϖi−1j+1−2ω1ϖi+1j+1+2ℓω2rij+1)(Z±ℑ^ij+1)+(−−σ(ρ)ρ−ω1ϖi−1j+12+ω2ϖij+1+3ω1ϖi−1j+12+ℓω1ri+1j+1)(Z±ℑ^i+1j+1),=±(−σ(ρ)ρ−3ω1ϖi−1j+12−ω2ϖij+1+ω1ϖi+1j+12+ℓω1ri−1j+1)ℑi−1j+1±(2σ(ρ)ρ+2ω1ϖi−1j+1−2ω1ϖi+1j+1+2ℓω2rij+1)ℑ^ij+1±(−−σ(ρ)ρ−ω1ϖi−1j+12+ω2ϖij+1+3ω1ϖi−1j+12+ℓω1ri+1j+1)ℑi+1j+1+(ℓω1ri−1j+1+2ℓω2rij+1+ℓω1ri+1j+1)Z,±ℓ(ω1φi−1j+1+2ω2φij+1+ω1φi+1j+1)+(ℓω1ri−1j+1+2ℓω2rij+1+ℓω1ri+1j+1)Z,=(ℓω1ri−1j+1+2ℓω2rij+1+ℓω1ri+1j+1)(∥£εN,Mℑ^ij+1∥η+max{|ℑ^0j+1|,|ℑ^Nj+1|})∓ℓ(ω1φi−1j+1+2ω2φij+1+ω1φi+1j+1),`≥0,sincerj+1(zi)≥η>0.By Lemma 4.1, we obtain (Πij+1)±≥0,0≤i≤1. Hence, the required bound is obtained. □Lemma 4.3The local truncation error in space semi-discretization of the discrete problem(3.27)is given asmaxi,j|ℑ^j+1(zi)−ℑ^ij+1|≤Cℓ2,whereCis a constant independent ofεandℓ.ProofFrom the truncation error of Eq. (3.19):(4.1){ei−1′=dI^j+1(zi−1)dz−dI^i−1j+1dz=ℓ23d3I^j+1(zi)dz3−ℓ312d4I^j+1(zi)dz4+ℓ430d5I^j+1(ξi)dz5,ei′=dI^j+1(zi)dz−dI^ij+1dz=−ℓ26d3I^j+1(zi)dz3−ℓ4120d5I^j+1(ξi)dz5,ei+1′=dI^j+1(zi+1)dz−dI^i+1j+1dz=ℓ23d3I^j+1(zi)dz3+ℓ312d4I^j+1(zi)dz4+ℓ430d5I^j+1(ξi)dz5,where zi−1<ξ<zi+1.SubstitutingσεΞβ=ϖβj+1dℑ^βj+1dz+rβj+1ℑβj+1−φβj+1,β=i,i±1into Eq. (3.17) yields,(4.2)σε(ℑ^i−1j+1−2ℑ^ij+1+ℑ^i+1j+1)=ℓ2ω1(ϖi−1j+1dℑ^i−1j+1dz+ri−1j+1ℑ^i−1j+1−φi−1j+1)+2ℓ2ω2(ϖij+1dℑ^ij+1dz+rij+1ℑ^ij+1−φij+1)+ℓ2ω1(ϖi+1j+1dℑ^i+1j+1dz+ri+1j+1ℑ^i+1j+1−φi+1j+1).Considering the corresponding exact solution to Eq. (4.2):(4.3)σε(ℑ^j+1(zi−1)−2ℑ^j+1(zi)+ℑ^j+1(zi+1))=ℓ2ω1ϖj+1(zi−1)dℑ^j+1(zi−1)dz+ℓ2ω1(rj+1(zi−1)ℑ^j+1(zi−1)−φj+1(zi−1))+2ℓ2ω2(ϖj+1(zi)dℑ^j+1(zi)dz+rj+1(zi)ℑ^j+1(zi))−2ℓ2ω2gj+1(zi)+ℓ2ω1(ϖj+1(zi+1)dℑ^j+1(zi+1)dz+rj+1(zi+1)ℑ^j+1(zi+1)−φj+1(zi+1)).Subtracting Eq. (4.2) from Eq. (4.3) and denoting eβ=ℑj+1(zβ)−ℑ^βj+1, for β=i,i±1 yields(4.4)(σε−ℓ2ω1ri−1j+1)ei−1+(−2σε−2ℓ2ω2rij+1)ei+(σε−ℓ2ω1ri+1j+1)ei+1=ℓ2(ω1ϖi−1j+1ei−1′+2ω2ϖij+1ei′+ω1ϖi+1j+1ei+1′).Inserting Eq. (4.1) in Eq. (4.4) gives(4.5)(σε−ℓ2ω1ri−1j+1)ei−1+(−2σε−2ℓ2ω2rij+1)ei+(σε−ℓ2ω1ri+1j+1)ei+1=ℓ43(ω1ϖi−1j+1−ω2ϖij+1+ω1ϖi+1j+1)d3ℑj+1(zi)dz3+ℓ512(−ω1ϖi−1j+1+ω1ϖi+1j+1)d4ℑj+1(zi)dz4+ℓ660(2ω1ϖi−1j+1−ω2ϖij+1+2ω1ϖi+1j+1)d5ℑj+1(ξi)dz5.Using the expressions ϖi−1=ϖi−ℓϖi′+ℓ22!ϖ(2)(ξi) and ϖi+1=ϖi+ℓϖi′+ℓ22!ϖ(2)(ξi) in Eq. (4.5):(4.6)(σε−ℓ2ω1ri−1j+1)ei−1+(−2σε−2ℓ2ω2rij+1)ei+(σε−ℓ2ω1ri+1j+1)ei+1=Ei(ℓ),where Ei(ℓ)=ℓ43(2ω1−ω2)ϖij+1d3ℑj+1(zi)dz3+O(ℓ6). Hence, for the choice of ω1+ω2=1/2, we obtain Ei(ℓ)=O(ℓ4).The matrix representation of Eq. (4.6) is(4.7)(Γ−Ω)⊤=E^,where Γ=trid(−σε,2σε,−σε), Ω=trid(ℓ2ω1ri−1j+1,2ℓ2ω1rij+1,ℓ2ω1ri+1j+1),⊤=[e1,e2,⋯,eN−1]t and E^=[−E1(ℓ),−E2(ℓ),⋯,−EN−1(ℓ)]t.Following [31], it can be shown that(4.8)∥⊤∥≤Cℓ2×O(ℓ4)=Cℓ2,where C is a constant, independent of ℓ and ε. □Theorem 4.1Letℑ(z,t)be the solution of problem(3.4)at each grid point(zi,tj+1)andℑ^ij+1be its approximate solution obtained by the proposed scheme given in Eq. (3.27). Then, the error estimate for the fully discrete method is given bymaxi,j|ℑ(zi,tj+1)−ℑ^ij+1|≤(C)(τ+ℓ2).ProofBy combining the result of Lemmas 3.2 and 4.3 the required bound is obtained. □
